javascript: JavaScript (JS) is a lightweight, interpreted, or just-in-time compiled programming language with first-class functions.
typescript: TypeScript is a strongly typed programming language that builds on JavaScript, giving you better tooling at any scale.
html: HyperText Markup Language (HTML) is the standard markup language for documents designed to be displayed in a web browser.
css: Cascading Style Sheets (CSS) is a style sheet language used for describing the presentation of a document written in a markup language such as HTML.
react: React is a free and open-source front-end JavaScript library for building user interfaces based on UI components.
node.js: Node.js is an open-source, cross-platform, back-end JavaScript runtime environment that runs on the V8 engine and executes JavaScript code outside a web browser.
git: Git is a distributed version control system for tracking changes in source code during software development.
terminal: A terminal emulator is a program that emulates a video terminal within some other display architecture.
npm: npm is a package manager for the JavaScript programming language maintained by npm, Inc.
webpack: Webpack is a static module bundler for modern JavaScript applications.
babel: Babel is a free and open-source JavaScript transpiler that is used to convert ECMAScript 2015+ code into backwards compatible JavaScript code.
eslint: ESLint is a static code analysis tool for identifying problematic patterns found in JavaScript code.
prettier: Prettier is an opinionated code formatter.
react router: React Router is a standard library for routing in React applications.
react hooks: React Hooks are functions that let you hook into React state and lifecycle features from function components.
express.js: Express.js, or simply Express, is a back end web application framework for building RESTful APIs with Node.js.
rest api: Representational State Transfer (REST) is a software architectural style that defines a set of constraints to be used for creating web services.
postman: Postman is an API platform for building and using APIs.
insomnia: Insomnia is a cross-platform API client for GraphQL, REST, and gRPC.
figma: Figma is a vector graphics editor and prototyping tool which is primarily web-based.
adobe photoshop: Adobe Photoshop is a raster graphics editor developed and published by Adobe Inc.
trello: Trello is a web-based, Kanban-style, list-making application.
agile: Agile software development is an approach to software development under which requirements and solutions evolve through the collaborative effort of self-organizing and cross-functional teams and their customers.
scrum: Scrum is an agile project management framework used primarily for software development.
lean: Lean is a methodology that focuses on minimizing waste within manufacturing systems while simultaneously maximizing productivity.
gtd: Getting Things Done (GTD) is a time management method.
teamwork: Teamwork is the collaborative effort of a group to achieve a common goal or to complete a task in the most effective and efficient way.
toggl: Toggl Track is a time tracking software.
heroku: Heroku is a platform as a service (PaaS) that enables developers to build, run, and operate applications entirely in the cloud.
namecheap: Namecheap, Inc. is an ICANN-accredited domain registrar and technology company.
sass: Sass (Syntactically Awesome Style Sheets) is a preprocessor scripting language that is interpreted or compiled into Cascading Style Sheets (CSS).
bootstrap: Bootstrap is a free and open-source CSS framework directed at responsive, mobile-first front-end web development.
handlebars: Handlebars.js is a popular templating engine that is powerful, simple to use with a large community.
flex: CSS Flexible Box Layout, commonly known as Flexbox, is a CSS3 web layout model.
grid: CSS Grid Layout is a two-dimensional layout system for CSS.
responsive design: Responsive web design (RWD) is an approach to web design that makes web pages render well on a variety of devices and window or screen sizes.
adaptive design: Adaptive web design (AWD) is an approach to web design that creates different versions of a web page to better fit the user's device.
bem: BEM (Block Element Modifier) is a front-end methodology for developing websites.
web applications: A web application (web app) is application software that runs on a web server, unlike computer-based software programs that are run locally on the operating system (OS) of the device.
user interface design: User interface (UI) design is the process designers use to build interfaces in software or computerized devices.
ші: Штучний інтелект (ШІ) — галузь інформатики, що вивчає можливість створення штучного розуму, тобто створення інтелектуальних машин.
хмарні сервіси: Хмарні обчислення — модель надання зручного доступу до спільного пула конфігурованих обчислювальних ресурсів (наприклад, мережі, серверів, сховищ, додатків та послуг).
api: Програмний інтерфейс додатка (API) — це набір визначених методів комунікації між різними програмними компонентами.
cloudflare: Cloudflare, Inc. — американська веб-інфраструктура та безпекова компанія, що надає послуги доставки вмісту та захисту від DDoS-атак.
aws: Amazon Web Services (AWS) — дочірня компанія Amazon, що надає на платформі на запит хмарні обчислювальні платформи та API.
web technologies: Web technologies refer to the means by which computers communicate with each other using markup languages and multimedia packages.
full stack: Full-stack development refers to the development of both front-end (client-side) and back-end (server-side) portions of an application.
front-end developer: A front-end developer is a type of programmer that implements web designs through programming languages such as HTML, CSS, and JavaScript.
junior developer js: Junior JavaScript Developer is an entry-level position focused on developing web applications using JavaScript and related technologies.
m.a. of economics: Master of Arts in Economics is a postgraduate degree focusing on economic theory, econometrics, and applied economics.
m.a. of civil engineering: Master of Arts in Civil Engineering is a postgraduate degree focusing on advanced concepts in civil engineering.
it-brains: IT-Brains is an educational institution offering courses in full-stack development with JavaScript, React, and Node.js.
sumy national agrarian university: Sumy National Agrarian University is a higher educational institution in Sumy, Ukraine.
gradex plus construction: Gradex Plus Construction is a construction company.
euroecobud construction company: Euroecobud Construction company is a construction company.
kyivbudengineering llc: Kyivbudengineering LLC is a construction company.
vscode: Visual Studio Code (VSCode) is a free source-code editor made by Microsoft for Windows, Linux and macOS.
development tools: Development tools are software applications that help developers write, test, and debug code more efficiently.
tech skills: Technical skills are abilities and knowledge needed to perform specific tasks in software development and IT.
related technologies: Related technologies are tools and frameworks that complement the main technology stack.
soft skills: Soft skills are non-technical skills that relate to how you work, including communication, teamwork, and problem-solving.
github: GitHub is a platform for version control and collaboration using Git, allowing developers to manage and store their code.
linkedin: LinkedIn is a business and employment social media platform for networking and career development.
telegram: Telegram is a cloud-based instant messaging and VoIP service that allows users to send messages and make voice/video calls.
bila tserkva: Bila Tserkva is a city in Kyiv Oblast, Ukraine, located on the Rosava River.
kiev: Kyiv is the capital and largest city of Ukraine, located on the Dnipro River.
sumy: Sumy is a city in northeastern Ukraine, known for its educational institutions and industrial heritage.
ukraine: Ukraine is a country in Eastern Europe, the second-largest in Europe after Russia.
bank app: A bank web application provides users with convenient access to various banking services online.
business card site: A business card site is a small, simple website that provides basic information about a company or individual.
twitter: Twitter is a social media platform for posting short messages and engaging in public conversations.
resume: A resume is a document that summarizes a person's education, work experience, and skills for job applications.
ajax: AJAX (Asynchronous JavaScript and XML) is a set of web development techniques using many web technologies on the client-side to create asynchronous web applications.
web api: Web API (Application Programming Interface) is an application programming interface for either a web server or a web browser.
functional programming: Functional programming is a programming paradigm where programs are constructed by applying and composing functions.
object oriented programming: Object-oriented programming (OOP) is a programming paradigm based on the concept of 'objects', which can contain data and code: data in the form of fields, and code, in the form of procedures.
document object model (dom): The Document Object Model (DOM) is a cross-platform and language-independent interface that treats an XML or HTML document as a tree structure.
